Home › FAQs › Postgre

Postgre

Last updated: Mar 15, 2024

Table of Contents

  • PostgreSQL Cheatsheet
  • 1) array_to_string : array to str
  • 2) cast : type transform
  • 2’) to_char : type transform
  • 3) Concat : merge str
  • 4) substring : get sub str
  • 5) row_number() : get row num (user defined)
  • 6) array_agg : make elements to array
  • 7) array : transform result to array
  • 8) ceil(num) : get int, (bigger one)
  • 9) floor(num) : get int, (smaller one)
  • 10) round(numeric,int)
  • 11) age(timestamp1,timestamp2)
  • 12) date_trunc(text,timestamp)
  • 13) date_part(text,timestamp)

PostgreSQL Cheatsheet

  • Ref
    • https://www.163.com/dy/article/G0F041FP0538QE50.html
    • https://www.itread01.com/content/1546958947.html
    • https://www.runoob.com/postgresql/postgresql-syntax.html

1) array_to_string : array to str

1select array_to_string(array[1,2,3], ',');
2# 123

2) cast : type transform

1select cast(id as varchar) from student;

2’) to_char : type transform

1select to_char(write_date, 'yyyy-MM-dd hh24:MI:ss') from student

3) Concat : merge str

1select
2concat('student:id=', cast(s.id as varchar), 'name:',s.name, 'class:',ci.name)
3from student as s

4) substring : get sub str

1select substring('abcd',1,2); 
2# ab

5) row_number() : get row num (user defined)

1select
2ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id desc) AS sequence_number,
3id,name
4from
5student

6) array_agg : make elements to array

1select array_agg(name order by name asc) from student

7) array : transform result to array

1SELECT array(SELECT "name" FROM student);

8) ceil(num) : get int, (bigger one)

1SELECT ceil(35.7)
2#36

9) floor(num) : get int, (smaller one)

1SELECT floor(35.7)
2#35

10) round(numeric,int)

11) age(timestamp1,timestamp2)

12) date_trunc(text,timestamp)

1select date_trunc('day',current_timestamp),date_trunc('hour',current_timestamp);
2--       date_trunc      |      date_trunc      
3-- ------------------------+------------------------
4-- 2018-09-16 00:00:00+08 | 2018-09-16 11:00:00+08

13) date_part(text,timestamp)

1select date_part('hour',now()),date_part('minute',now()),date_part('month',now());
2date_part | date_part | date_part 
3-- -----------+-----------+-----------
4--         11 |        10 |        9
